items.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with client

children.content: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

gallery.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

thumbnail.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Man dies on APY Lands after large fight breaks out, police say

children.content: A man has died after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, with police investigating whether his death was the result of a medical episode.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: Time to bite the bullet? Unpopular decision looms over $600m Aboriginal gallery

children.content: Adelaide's stalled Aboriginal cultural centre has a dubious future on a site with a troubled past. The Malinauskas government faces criticism whatever it chooses to do, writes state political reporter Thomas Kelsall.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a future use of the Lot Fourteen site.

items.title: 'Fierce attack': Victim refuses to clean antisemitic graffiti off property

children.content: The victim of an antisemitic attack says he doesn't want to clean up offensive graffiti sprayed on his Adelaide property, because he wants the public to see his family was targeted

gallery.altText: a man stands infront of a graffiti sprayed fence, Nazi symbols have been blurred

thumbnail.altText: a man stands infront of a graffiti sprayed fence, Nazi symbols have been blurred

items.title: One dead after suspected carbon monoxide poisoning in Mount Gambier

children.content: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

image.altText: Emergency services vehicles in Mount Gambier.

items.title: Artist fighting Temu to reclaim her mosaic design

children.content: A South Australian artist is fighting drop-ship company Temu over its selling of her mosaic design on rugs. But how did the artwork get there in the first place?

image.altText: A woman poses with her original mosaic work and its copy-cat that's been printed onto a rug sold on a drop ship website.

items.title: 'Sacred' Coorong wetlands bracing for impact of bird flu

children.content: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

articles.title: First native bird found with H5 bird flu, marking 'devastating' new reality

children.content: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

image.altText: A greater crested tern on South Africa's western cape.

gallery.altText: Drone picture of the Coorong, showing lagoons and sand dunes.
Two black swams swim on a lake in the Coorong.

thumbnail.altText: Drone picture of the Coorong, showing lagoons and sand dunes.
Two black swams swim on a lake in the Coorong.

items.title: Workers waiting a year for entitlements from GFG may never be paid

children.content: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

image.altText: A man in a yellow high vis shirt and a beany both of which say Ferretti International.

items.title: Claim woman's death caused by Telstra outage is 'incorrect', SA Police say

children.content: South Australia's police chief says there is "nothing to suggest" the death of a person in a regional hospital was caused by any failure to connect to Triple Zero (000) during the Telstra outage, following an investigation into the matter.

image.altText: Close up of mobile phone with SOS message due to Telstra outage

items.title: Broken Hill's long legacy of lead exposure

children.content: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

image.altText: A mother with dyed red hair on a chair with a toddler with red hair on the lap

items.title: Adelaide Crows player in collision involving pedestrian

children.content: Police are investigating the collision which took place on Port Road at Woodville on Friday morning.

image.altText: crows players Josh worrell, text walker and others walking off field

items.title: Getting guernseys reverses fortunes of one of the most remote footy clubs

children.content: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.

image.altText: The new design of the guernsey of the Fregon Bulldogs football club.

items.title: Hardwick lashes 'peanut' football fans after fiery run-in with spectator

children.content: Damien Hardwick clashes with Crows fans at Adelaide Oval, as Jeremy Cameron's injury timeline is revealed.

image.altText: hardwick2

items.title: These homes are classed as 'affordable', but there's a catch

children.content: Governments across Australia are spending billions to boost the number of rentals available to people on low and middle incomes. A Four Corners investigation found many of these homes were unaffordable for those who needed them most, particularly single-income households.

gallery.altText: A composite of real estate listing photos of two modern luxury apartments.

thumbnail.altText: A composite of real estate listing photos of two modern luxury apartments.

items.title: Jana Armstrong's former partner remanded in custody on murder charge

children.content: Toowoomba man Dharminder Singh, 48, whose former partner, Jana Armstrong's body was discovered on the weekend, has appeared in a Darling Downs court charged with murder.

image.altText: A woman lovingly holds a small baby

items.title: Albanese has the Coalition exactly where he wants it

children.content: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem

children.content: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

gallery.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

thumbnail.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

items.title: Change to international travel as digital arrival cards roll out

children.content: A digital alternative to incoming passenger cards filled out by everyone entering Australia is to be implemented in the next two years.

image.altText: Two male passengers wheeling suitcases in an airport terminal.

items.title: FIFA boss hints at World Cup expansion talks, defends hydration breaks

children.content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino believes this year's World Cup is proof that expanding the tournament was the right move.

image.altText: Gianni Infantino smiles and points with his right hand

items.title: 'He continues to inspire us': Richard Scolyer's family pay emotional tribute

children.content: Thousands have gathered for Richard Scolyer's state memorial service, with the leading melanoma pathologist remembered as an esteemed researcher and family man.

articles.title: Tour de Cure reflects on Scolyer's legacy ahead of memorial service

children.content: As Mark Beretta rode his bike up a steep Tasmanian mountain in March with all his might, it was none other than Richard Scolyer who overtook him.

image.altText: Mark Beretta and Richard Scolyer in black lyra, smiling as they stand near a paddock in Tasmania.

gallery.altText: Portrait head and shoulders Richard Scolyer slight smile wearing a suit

thumbnail.altText: Portrait head and shoulders Richard Scolyer slight smile wearing a suit

items.title: Businesses caught between Labor's trust tax and stamp duty

children.content: Small businesses affected by the change are in limbo as states decide whether to waive stamp duty fees, with industry groups warning relief will be limited unless they sign on.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit standing in front of an Australian flag.

items.title: ASX slips as oil price jumps, Telstra warns of post-outage scams

children.content: The ASX has slipped on renewed war tensions, while Telstra has warned customers about a spate of postage scamming. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Ways to reduce your drinking without quitting altogether

children.content: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

gallery.altText: A group of young people drinking and laughing

thumbnail.altText: A group of young people drinking and laughing

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: History quiz: Test your recall on Tay Tay and the Tour de France

children.content: How much do you know about long-past British monarchs, the Salem witch trials and Nintendo games of the 80s? Find out with this week's quiz.

image.altText: Close up of Taylor Swift looking serious on stage in sparkly bodysuit

items.title: New exhibition challenges visitors to slow down and put their phones away

children.content: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

image.altText: Palomita Soledad by Nadia Hernandez featuring large cutout letters adhered to a large canvas, hanging in a gallery.

items.title: Floods devastate Bangladesh as dozens killed

children.content: Days of torrential monsoon rain have triggered major flooding, leaving millions stranded and facing food supply shortages in the south-east of the country.

gallery.altText: Residents wade through a waterlogged street after heavy monsoon rains in Dhaka, Bangladesh.

thumbnail.altText: Residents wade through a waterlogged street after heavy monsoon rains in Dhaka, Bangladesh.

items.title: Australians paying 'unjustified' fees to pharmacies, report says

children.content: Taxpayers are missing out on cheaper medicines due to backroom deals by one of Australia's most powerful lobby groups, according to a new report.

image.altText: A script counter at a pharmacy, with an unidentifiable man waiting in line.

items.title: Bangkok pub fire kills at least 27 people and injures 63 others

children.content: A huge fire engulfed a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people before firefighters brought the blaze under control.

image.altText: Rescuers stand outside a closed pub with a bright, colourful facade and the words 'Blend 285' on the front.

items.title: Melbourne cafe catches fire three days after alleged drive-by shooting

children.content: Police are treating a fire at an Essendon North cafe as suspicious, just days after the business was sprayed with bullets.

image.altText: Police tape seen at an Essendon North cafe.

items.title: Proposal to cut blanket ban on climbing at Mount Arapiles Dyurrite

children.content: Climbing Victoria says proposed changes to the Mount Arapiles Dyurrite management plan will be a compromise between climbers and traditional owners built on "trust".

image.altText: Large rocks above Wimmera plains

items.title: Camp to boost female firefighter participation ditched after one year

children.content: The free camp ran over a weekend in June 2024 for students in years 9 and 10, exploring firefighting activities, road crashes, swiftwater rescues, and physical fitness training.

gallery.altText: A close-up of a fire truck, with 'fire' written on red paint

thumbnail.altText: A close-up of a fire truck, with 'fire' written on red paint

avatar.alt: A portrait shot of Eden standing next to a tree.

items.title: Convicted paedophile to argue multiple-personality evidence unlawful

children.content: A father jailed for abusing two children is appealing against his conviction, arguing evidence given by the multiple personalities of the same witness was unlawful.

image.altText: A court sketch of a man with his face blurred.

items.title: Illegal camping fine of $345 divides coastal community

children.content: A crackdown on illegal camping in Noosa, prompted by hundreds of complaints, has sparked outrage locally, with many arguing fines will not solve the issue.

image.altText: police talking to people illegally camping at Noosa

items.title: Worn-down 'jewel' needs some love ahead of World Heritage Bid

children.content: Once a booming gold rush town, Walhalla is preparing for a different kind of boom, but first, it needs critical government investment in infrastructure.

image.altText: A small village of heritage buildings, surrounded by autumn trees, seen from up high

items.title: Agonising finish sees Lee denied another Scottish Open title

children.content: Min Woo Lee comes close to emulating his feats from 2021 but is denied by a bogey-free run-in from eventual winner Tom Kim.

image.altText: Min Woo Lee holds a golf ball in his right hand
